text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. Many teams use SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This framework helps ensure that everyone understands exactly what is expected. Once the main goal is set, you can break it into smaller milestones. These are key checkpoints that show progress over time. For each milestone, define specific deliverables, such as a report, a prototype, or a completed task. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. All team members and anyone affected by the project should agree on the goals and milestones. This prevents misunderstandings and keeps the project on track from start to finish.
